title: BSCI:414 Lab 1--Benchling and PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5vcf","text":"Lab 1 Overview","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ev50v","text":"\tDiscuss and Begin Lab Notebooks in Benchling","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1pgan","text":"\tImport and Explore DNA in Benchling","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7nolb","text":"\tMake primers in Benchling","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cmc57","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Discuss features of a good lab notebook e.g. dates, navigation.
Sign into Benchling, join and find project "USG BSCI:414 Fall 2020 Lab".

Begin your own lab notebook by: Creating folder entitled "FirstName LastNameLab Notebook" under Projects/USG BSCI:414 Fall 2020 Lab/" Clicking "+" sign and "Entry>Blank Entry". Entitle this the same as the folder e.g. Harley King Lab Notebook" Begin by including a link to this protocol. Create a Benchling Lab Notebook using Entry>Blank Entry
Overview of Benchling sequences Importing DNA Copying/pasting DNA Understanding DNA features Undo history

Students Import DNA sequences Create a new folder called "FirstName LastName Lab 1" under "Projects/USG BSCI:414 Fall 2020 Lab/Lab 1" Import a DNA sequence and attempt the following: Delete part of the DNA. Undo the deletion. Add bases to the DNA. Delete the additional bases. Do crazy stuff and then use the "Undo History" to reset the plasmid back to its original state.

Into your lab 1 folder, copy the sequence "Addgene_SARS_Spike_pGBW-m4136458" from "Projects/USG BSCI:414 Fall 2020 Lab/". Put your name in front of the sequence e.g. "HarleyKingcopy_Addgene_SARS_Spike_pGBW-m4136458". Select a sequence from this plasmid < 3kb Select primer icon from right side then "CREATE PRIMERS" then "Wizard". The target range can be any shift-selected bp region or you can enter numbers like 0 to 3000. click "Generate Primers". With luck, a few primer options will appear on the next screen. Notce that each contains a forward "FWD" and reverse "REV" primer. Both are necessary for successful PCR. Select one "FWD" and one "REV" primer with a checkmark. Select "Save Selected Primers". In the box that pops up, save them to your Lab 1 folder. Return to "Sequence Map". Scroll through the sequence and find your primers. Rename the primers by right clicking>Edit and changing the name. Shift select between the primers. How many bp is the fragment? Note this in your lab notebook. Select primer icon, then "Create Primers" then "Wizard". Select 1 "FWD" primer and 1 "REV" primer.
In your lab notebook, use "@ mention" to link the name of your plasmid. Also link the renamed primers. Note how many bp your PCR fragment is.
creator: Harley King
